title: Set up MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages deploy + Diataxis skeleton
description:
Goal: Foundation for the Diataxis docs site. Get the publishing pipeline working end-to-end against placeholder content; subsequent migration beads (A2-A5) fill the quadrants.

Files: mkdocs.yml, requirements.txt (build env), .github/workflows/docs.yml, docs/index.md (4-quadrant landing), docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md (placeholder quadrant landings).

Steps:
1. Author mkdocs.yml with Material theme + mkdocs-include-markdown plugin (glob auto-discovery enabled). Enable search, dark mode, navigation tabs.
2. requirements.txt: mkdocs-material + mkdocs-include-markdown-plugin pinned to current versions.
3. GitHub Actions workflow: build on push to main, deploy to gh-pages branch.
4. Skeleton landing pages: root index.md orients readers by intent (new? → Tutorials / solving a problem? → How-to / looking up a fact? → Reference / want to understand why? → Explanation), each quadrant index.md is a stub TOC.
5. Configure repo Pages settings: source = gh-pages branch, custom domain optional.

Acceptance:
- mkdocs build succeeds locally.
- GH Actions deploys on push to main.
- Published page resolves; the 4-quadrant nav is navigable.
- Stub landing pages render without broken links.

Diataxis-discipline note (from R1 drawer at claim time): ensure index.md orientation copy follows the 4-quadrant intent pattern Daniele Procida recommends (NOT a hierarchical product-feature nav).

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead.
close_reason: MkDocs Material + GH Pages deploy + Diataxis 4-quadrant skeleton landed (commit 4f3cd06; merged 30a5b5c). All four quadrants land with at-least-one real page per R1 C3 (no empty stubs): Tutorials = 2 walkthroughs git-mv'd; How-to = install.md from README; Reference = session-startup auto-included via mkdocs-include-markdown demonstrating C1; Explanation = §1 mental-model lifted from manual.md. mkdocs build --strict exits 0. Unblocks loom-9z1.3-.6 (A2-A5 quadrant migrations), loom-9z1.8 (B1 doc-surface check), loom-9z1.9 (/audit-project --check=docs). manual.md retire deferred to A6 (loom-9z1.7).

title: Diataxis: principles, fail points, loom-specific accommodations (research)
description:
Goal: Establish a locked understanding of Diataxis (the documentation framework) BEFORE any restructure work begins. Every implementation/migration bead in this epic depends on this drawer.

Define the question:
- What does each Diataxis quadrant (Tutorials, How-to, Reference, Explanation) actually require — voice, audience, depth, what to keep OUT?
- Where do real-world Diataxis adoptions fail? (Common patterns to validate: tutorial creep into how-to; reference padding into explanation; four-quadrant nav becoming a navigation trap; primitive-as-reference duplication; getting-started tutorial bloat.)
- What loom-specific accommodations do we need? (Markdown-heavy infra repo where SKILLs are already reference-shaped; canonical-skills with mkdocs-include-markdown glob; a single-author meta-project with no external users yet but designed to be picked up.)

Steps:
1. Search palace + bd memories for prior Diataxis art (probably none).
2. Fetch authoritative external docs: diataxis.fr (canonical site by Daniele Procida); published critiques + case studies; any blog posts about Diataxis fail modes.
3. Synthesize.

Output: decision drawer in loom/decisions naming
(a) discipline rules per quadrant (voice / audience / what to include / what to keep OUT),
(b) documented fail patterns + how to avoid them,
(c) loom-specific accommodations (canonical-skills, glob auto-discovery, single-author dynamics).

Recipe shape: research-a-bead. Drawer is the deliverable.

Blocks: A1, D1 (which transitively blocks the rest of the epic).
close_reason: R1 research output filed in loom/decisions (drawer DIATAXIS DISCIPLINE — R1 RESEARCH OUTPUT). Synthesized 4-quadrant verbatim-Procida discipline rules + 10 fail-pattern catalog (F1-F10) + 6 loom accommodations (C1-C6). Unblocks A1 (loom-9z1.2), A2-A5, B1, C1, D1.

title: Diataxis docs restructure: MkDocs Material + canonical-skills + drift defenses
description:
Rebuild loom's docs as a Diataxis-shaped MkDocs Material site published via GitHub Pages. Skills/commands/agents/hooks remain canonical (single source of truth); docs site curates and links via mkdocs-include-markdown glob auto-discovery. Process integration (recipe phase B1 doc-surface check) + drift detection (/audit-project --check=docs) defend the alignment.

Phase 1 (this epic): loom's own docs.
Phase 2 (D1's plan + future child beads): extract pattern as template loom-managed projects can adopt via /audit-project.

Locked decisions captured in conversation 2026-05-03:
- Scope: Option 3 (loom first, then template-ize)
- Tooling: MkDocs Material + GitHub Pages
- Source-of-truth: option 1 (skills canonical, docs link via include-markdown)
- Process: B1 doc-surface check in bead-lifecycle-shell
- Drift detection: extend /audit-project with --check=docs (system + beads + MemPalace > docs precedence)
- Glob auto-discovery for primitive includes (no nav.yml maintenance)
- 10 beads under one epic

The full design will be filed as a decision drawer at R1's close + supplemented at D1's close. The child beads' descriptions will be enriched with detailed per-page content specs at claim time, sourced from R1's drawer.

title: Drop disable-model-invocation flag from activity recipe SKILL.mds + shell
description: The six activity recipe SKILL.mds (bugfix/feature/refactor/research/cleanup/docs-a-bead) and bead-lifecycle-shell carry 'disable-model-invocation: true' in frontmatter. This makes the Skill tool refuse to load them ('cannot be used with Skill tool due to disable-model-invocation'), which blocks the loom-1ab router from dispatching to recipes via Skill(<recipe>) — the canonical handoff path. The existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands also say 'Invoke the X skill' and would hit the same wall (untested). FIX: remove 'disable-model-invocation: true' from frontmatter of all 7 SKILL.mds. Recipes are explicitly invoked via slash command (or via the router); the flag was belt-and-suspenders against accidental auto-fire. Descriptions are already narrow and workflow-mode off/light gating provides additional defense. ACCEPTANCE: 7 SKILL.mds edited; Skill(<any-recipe>) loads cleanly; existing /bugfix-a-bead and /research-a-bead slash commands work end-to-end. See loom/decisions drawer 0e40c06 (2026-05-03).
close_reason: Removed disable-model-invocation:true from 6 recipe SKILL.mds + bead-lifecycle-shell. Updated body text + manual.md. Unblocks loom-1ab router. commit c44d7cb.

title: loom: /wrap-up final step — deploy via install.sh when in loom checkout
description: When working on loom, the /wrap-up command should auto-detect the loom checkout (presence of install.sh + settings.snippet.json + skills/ + commands/ at pwd root) and run a deploy step as its final action. The deploy step needs to (a) mkdir any missing ~/.claude/skills/<new>/ parent dirs, (b) rm dangling symlinks owned by loom (target points into $LOOM_ROOT but file no longer exists), then (c) ./install.sh. This compensates for two install.sh gaps documented today in MemPalace drawer 'install.sh — TWO RENAME-DEPLOY GAPS FOUND' (loom/decisions, 2026-05-03). Until install.sh itself absorbs the fix (separate bead, P3), this addendum keeps loom self-deploying after every wrap-up. Edit only commands/wrap-up.md; the loom-detection check guards the step from firing in non-loom projects.
close_reason: Added section 6 to commands/wrap-up.md: detects loom checkout, mkdir's missing skill subdirs, prunes dangling loom-owned symlinks, runs ./install.sh. Section is no-op in non-loom projects. Cross-refs the install.sh gap drawer for lineage. Will collapse to plain ./install.sh once install.sh fix lands (separate bead).

title: v2: router — /working-a-bead becomes selector that dispatches by bead.type
description: commands/working-a-bead.md becomes a router. Logic: takes bead-id (optional --recipe=<name> override), runs bd show, scores against the 6 recipes by bead.type field + description heuristics. Routes to the matching recipe. On ambiguity (bead matches 2+ recipes plausibly): list ambiguous candidates with one-line 'because' for each and prompt user to pick or use --recipe=<name> override (Frank's locked decision 6, 2026-05-02). Default routing: type=bug → bugfix; type=feature → feature; type=epic → planning (TBD); type=task → check description keywords (refactor/cleanup/docs/research) with fallback to bugfix.
close_reason: Router shipped at commands/working-a-bead.md. Dispatches by bead.type with keyword scoring for tasks, ambiguity prompt on ties, --recipe override, epic guard. Skill(<recipe>) dispatch enabled by loom-7z1. End-to-end smoke test deferred to next session. commit da939e6.

title: v2: research-a-bead recipe
description: skills/research-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: define the question → search prior art (mempalace_search + kg_query + bd memories + diary_read) → fetch authoritative docs (context7 if library; web if needed) → synthesize → file findings as drawer + KG triples + optional follow-up beads. No code, no worktree (usually). Captures the discipline that was implicit in the deploy-day MemPalace search habit.
close_reason: research-a-bead recipe authored; sibling to bugfix-a-bead under epic loom-0y6

title: v2: feature-a-bead recipe
description: skills/feature-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: brainstorm (superpowers/beadpowers) → writing-plans (multi-task) → TDD on NEW behavior (the contract) → integration test → bug-class equivalent for negative cases. Differs from bugfix: no symptom to reproduce; the RED test pins desired behavior, not failure. References the shared bead-lifecycle-shell for claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: refactor-a-bead recipe
description: skills/refactor-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope → write characterization tests if missing (pin current behavior) → refactor → verify behavior preserved (all original tests pass) → close. Differs from bugfix: TDD inverted — tests come BEFORE the refactor and must KEEP passing. No new behavior; structural change only.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: rename working-a-bead → bugfix-a-bead + retitle for activity shape
description: Today working-a-bead is bug-shaped (forged during HAW deploy-day cleanup). Once the activity recipes land it should be one of N recipes the router selects among. Rename the skill directory + filename + frontmatter name, update internal references in router + session-startup hand-off + manual + walkthrough. Shell out the bug-specific middle (reproduce → RED → minimal GREEN → bug-class coverage → enshrined-test sweep) — the shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill already owns claim/isolate/verify/close/capture.
close_reason: Renamed skills/working-a-bead/ → skills/bugfix-a-bead/ + commands/working-a-bead.md → commands/bugfix-a-bead.md. SKILL.md rewritten as slim variable middle (M1-M5) with phases A/B/C/D delegated to bead-lifecycle-shell. All refs swept across install/uninstall/README/CLAUDE/manual/walkthrough/session-startup/shell/agents. workflow.json set to light. Commit bc841d7 merged to main as 47-line diff. Unblocks loom-1ab router and the 5 sibling recipes.

title: v2: activity-shaped recipes + router (continued from HAW bng)
description: Continuation of the v2 work originally tracked in HAW epic hundred-acre-woods-bng. The shared bead-lifecycle-shell skill (was 7o9) shipped before migration. Remaining: 5 activity recipes (feature, refactor, research, cleanup, docs) + the /working-a-bead router that selects among them by bead.type/heuristics + final pass on docs/manual.md and docs/walkthrough.md to reflect the multi-recipe shape. Design + locked decisions captured in MemPalace drawer 'RECIPE SHAPES — ACTIVITY MATRIX' (hundred_acre_woods/decisions, 2026-05-02) — see also LOOM PROVENANCE drawer in loom/decisions wing for the full inheritance chain. Frank's locked decisions: 6 recipes (bug already exists as bugfix-shaped working-a-bead; rename pending), shared shell as separate skill (shipped), router goes straight to selector pattern not phased intro, full schema for state file with stage as first-class.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Plan: loom configures Diataxis docs for managed projects (research)
description:
Goal: Plan how loom should ship Diataxis-docs-as-a-template for OTHER repos that use loom (HAW, future projects). This is Phase 2 of the docs initiative — eat-our-own-dogfood first (A1-A6 + B1 + C1 = loom's own docs), THEN formalize the pattern as something loom-managed projects inherit.

Define the question:
- What does loom need to ship so other repos using loom can adopt Diataxis trivially?
- Audit-project extension: how does it detect / scaffold Diataxis structure for a new project? (E.g., new check item: 'project has docs/ with 4 quadrant subdirs?' If MISS, offer a scaffold.)
- New skill or command for docs-scaffold-generator?
- Templates: what does loom ship as the canonical Diataxis skeleton (mkdocs.yml template, GH Actions workflow template, docs/{tutorials,how-to,reference,explanation}/index.md placeholders, README pointer template)?
- Conventions: which loom-managed-project primitives auto-generate doc entries? (For HAW specifically: HAW's own skills/, commands/, agents/, hooks/ — does loom's audit recommend Diataxis-shaped docs for those? Or is that HAW's own choice?)
- The 'precedence rule when docs disagree with system/beads/MemPalace' — how does it transfer to managed projects (where loom is the meta and the project has its own MemPalace wing)?

Steps (research-a-bead recipe):
1. Search palace + bd memories: prior loom-managed-project decisions; HAW-side experience with docs.
2. Synthesize: what worked in A1-A6 + B1 + C1; what would generalize; what's loom-specific vs project-specific.
3. Draft drawer.

Output: decision drawer in loom/decisions naming the proposed surface for the template extraction. Optional: file follow-up implementation child beads (filed at D1 close-out per research-a-bead's M5 step). Implementation child beads are NOT part of THIS epic — D1's deliverable is the plan itself.

Acceptance:
- Drawer landed in loom/decisions.
- User reviews + signs off on the proposed surface.
- If implementation beads filed, they're priority-tagged appropriately as a successor epic (loom-XXX2).
- Plan respects loom's existing 'one bead = one branch = one worktree' + 'capture decisions in MemPalace' conventions.

Recipe shape: research-a-bead. Drawer is the deliverable. Depends on R1 (principles) and A6 (eat-our-own-dogfood completed).

title: /audit-project --check=docs: drift detection vs system + beads + MemPalace
description:
Goal: Extend /audit-project with a docs alignment check. Per the precedence rule (when docs disagree with system / beads / MemPalace, docs lose), the check reports doc fixes — never the other way around.

Files: skills/audit-project/SKILL.md (add --check=docs flag + checks), agents/project-onboarder.md (extend scan logic if needed).

Checks (v1, naive grep):
1. **Cardinality** — docs that count things ('All six commands have disable-model-invocation'); grep source-of-truth, compare. Catches the loom-469 class.
2. **Citation resolution** — every bead-ID pattern (loom-[a-z0-9]{3}), commit SHA, file path, drawer slug, slash command name in docs/ must resolve. `bd show` for beads, `git cat-file -e` for commits, filesystem checks for paths, mempalace_search for drawer slugs.
3. **Behavior claims** — docs that say 'X exists' / 'X does Y' against system reality. E.g., docs claim /feature-a-bead exists → check commands/feature-a-bead.md. Catches the loom-qj3 lying-doc bug class at audit time.
4. **Inclusion glob coverage** — every primitive on disk → has a doc page (auto-discovered); every doc page → corresponds to a real primitive. Symmetric check.
5. **Explanation-doc consistency** — explanation docs cite drawers; verify each via mempalace_get_drawer (or near-match via mempalace_search if slug is partial title).

Output: structured report — one line per drift item, tagged [DOC FIX], with what doc says, what reality says, suggested fix.

Flags:
- --apply-trivial — auto-apply count corrections + dead-bead-ID replacement (per supersedes-chain).
- Default behavior for loom-managed projects: docs check runs by default when /audit-project is invoked.

Out of scope (v2 if needed): semantic LLM-based claim extraction.

Acceptance:
- Running /audit-project --check=docs against current loom catches the loom-qj3 / loom-469 class of issues.
- No false positives for loom's healthy state (verified by running on a known-good commit).
- --apply-trivial cleanly fixes count / dead-bead drift without touching anything else.

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead. Depends on A1 + A2 (the Reference quadrant must exist for inclusion-glob check to have a target).

title: bead-lifecycle-shell phase B1: doc-surface audit sub-step
description:
Goal: Add a doc-surface check sub-step to phase B1 (verification before completion) of the bead-lifecycle-shell so docs stay aligned as work happens. Single insertion in the shell = every recipe inherits.

Files: skills/bead-lifecycle-shell/SKILL.md (add sub-step to B1), skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md (extend M2 orphan-reference grep to cover docs/).

Behavior added to B1: After diff-scope check, recipe asks:
1. Did the diff touch any primitive (skills/<name>/SKILL.md, commands/<name>.md, agents/<name>.md, hooks/<name>.sh)? If yes: docs site auto-picks up the body via include glob — quick chrome inspection only (intro paragraph above include, 'shipped in bead X' labels, cross-links). Usually 2 lines.
2. New primitive added or removed? Auto-handled by glob; nothing manual.
3. Did the diff change user-visible behavior described in non-reference docs (Tutorials / How-to / Explanation)? Recipe asks 'follow-up bead?' Default: file-later.

Plus: cleanup-a-bead's M2 orphan-reference grep extended to grep docs/ so removed primitives don't leave dead in-doc cross-links.

Acceptance:
- bead-lifecycle-shell SKILL.md updated (B1 has the new sub-step).
- cleanup-a-bead SKILL.md updated (M2 covers docs/).
- Smoke-tests for both pass.
- The next bead claimed after this lands exercises the new B1 step (verification: status line shows the doc-check sub-step).

Recipe shape: feature-a-bead (new behavior in shell). Depends on A1 (the doc surface must exist for the check to mean anything).

title: Retire manual.md, update README + CLAUDE.md cross-links
description:
Goal: After 9z1.3-9z1.6 land, manual.md is empty/redundant — remove it. Also wire up the navigation, resolve cross-quadrant xref: sentinels, and update README + CLAUDE.md cross-links. This is the central post-merge editorial pass.

Files: docs/manual.md (delete), docs/<quadrant>/_links.txt (delete after resolution), mkdocs.yml (nav), docs/**/*.md (xref rewrites), README.md, CLAUDE.md.

Scope expanded 2026-05-04 to include nav build + xref resolution per parallel-agent contract drawer (loom/decisions, drawer_loom_decisions_834f035cb836048b467afd5a). Original scope was retire manual.md + update cross-links; new scope folds in nav writing and xref sentinel resolution after the four parallel quadrant beads merge.

A. RETIRE MANUAL.MD
1. Verify manual.md has no content not migrated to 9z1.3-9z1.6.
2. git rm docs/manual.md.
3. Sweep repo for stale docs/manual.md references (grep -r) and update them.

B. BUILD NAV
1. Read mkdocs.yml nav stub.
2. For each quadrant, scan docs/<quadrant>/ for shipped pages.
3. Write nav block per quadrant: quadrant index first, then alphabetical (or per R1 hints).
4. Confirm nav references every .md under docs/ except _links.txt, internal/**, *.draft.md.

C. RESOLVE XREF SENTINELS
1. Concatenate all docs/<quadrant>/_links.txt → expected target set.
2. Verify every target file (+ anchor) exists. Hard error on miss.
3. Walk every .md under docs/. For each xref:<path>, compute relative path from source to target and rewrite.
4. Delete the four _links.txt files.

D. CROSS-LINK CLEANUP (original scope)
1. Rewrite README.md docs section to point at GitHub Pages site + 4-quadrant orientation.
2. Update CLAUDE.md any references to docs/manual.md to new doc paths or site URL.

E. VERIFY
- mkdocs build --strict — zero warnings.
- mkdocs link checker — zero broken refs.
- grep -r manual.md → zero hits.
- grep -r xref: docs/ → zero hits.
- README + CLAUDE.md current.

Acceptance:
- docs/manual.md does not exist on main.
- mkdocs build --strict green.
- Zero broken links site-wide.
- README + CLAUDE.md no longer reference manual.md.
- No xref: strings remain anywhere under docs/.

Recipe shape: cleanup-a-bead (orphan removal + central editorial pass).

title: Tutorials quadrant: move walkthroughs + author getting-started
description:
Goal: Move the two existing walkthroughs into docs/tutorials/ + author a NEW getting-started tutorial (zero to first shipped bead). The two existing walkthroughs are already Tutorial-shaped — main work is the new doc.

Diataxis discipline (refined by R1 drawer at claim): 2nd person, narrative, learning-by-doing. Takes a learner from zero to first concrete success. Minimum-required-information; no over-explaining. Honesty caveats where applicable. NOT 'experienced reader can skip ahead' (that's How-to).

Pages:
- docs/tutorials/index.md — quadrant landing
- docs/tutorials/getting-started.md — NEW: install + first session (distilled from README intro + manual §3 first-session content). Takes a new user from `git clone` to first shipped bead.
- docs/tutorials/bug-walkthrough.md — moved from docs/walkthrough.md
- docs/tutorials/feature-walkthrough.md — moved from docs/feature-walkthrough.md

Steps:
1. `git mv docs/walkthrough.md docs/tutorials/bug-walkthrough.md`
2. `git mv docs/feature-walkthrough.md docs/tutorials/feature-walkthrough.md`
3. Author docs/tutorials/getting-started.md from scratch (distilled from README + manual §3).
4. Update internal cross-links (some references in the two walkthroughs may need re-pathing; the two walkthroughs reference each other).

Acceptance:
- Each tutorial walks a real session end-to-end.
- Honesty caveats present where the recipe behavior is aspirational.
- New user could follow getting-started cold and ship a first bead by the end.
- Cross-link audits clean.
- No 'experienced reader can skip ahead' shortcuts (that voice belongs in How-to).

Recipe shape: refactor-a-bead (move existing) + feature-a-bead (new getting-started). Likely best to ship as one combined effort.
close_reason: Closed

title: Explanation quadrant: migrate manual.md §1 + split §4 + provenance
description:
Goal: Migrate the existing explanation content (manual.md §1 mental model + appendix provenance) into docs/explanation/, and SPLIT §4 'The recipes' between Reference (per-recipe spec) and Explanation (why six shapes; design tradeoffs; what was rejected). Reference half goes to A2; Explanation half lives here.

Diataxis discipline (refined by R1 drawer at claim): discursive, can be personal, 'let me tell you why.' Discusses design tradeoffs. NOT how-to (no step-by-step). NOT reference (no exhaustive lists). Each explanation has a thesis. Cross-references reference + how-to where readers might want concrete details; doesn't duplicate them.

Pages (revisit + finalize per R1 at claim time):
- docs/explanation/index.md — quadrant landing
- docs/explanation/mental-model.md — was §1 (four-axis memory model)
- docs/explanation/recipe-family.md — extracted from §4 (why six shapes, what was considered, what was rejected, the central inversions per shape)
- docs/explanation/workflow-modes.md — was §1's v1.5 sub-section + §2 v1.5 sub-section (full/light/off design)
- docs/explanation/provenance.md — current manual.md appendix (v1 → v1.5 → v2 lineage; loom's relationship to HAW)

Acceptance:
- Each explanation has a clear thesis.
- No exhaustive enumeration (links to Reference for those).
- No step-by-step (links to How-to or Tutorials for those).
- Cross-link audits clean (each explanation page references the related Reference / How-to / Tutorial pages).

Recipe shape: refactor-a-bead (with some splitting work — §4 splits across this bead and A2).
close_reason: Closed

title: How-to quadrant: migrate manual.md §3/§13/§14/§16 as task-oriented guides
description:
Goal: Migrate the existing how-to content (manual.md §3 daily session lifecycle + §13 bypass + §14 cookbook + §16 where-to-update-what) into docs/how-to/ as task-oriented guides per Diataxis discipline.

Diataxis discipline (refined by R1 drawer at claim): imperative voice, 'to do X follow these steps,' assumes context (no teaching), single task per guide. Each guide has a clear goal at the top and a clean exit. NOT learning narrative (that's Tutorials). NOT exhaustive lists (that's Reference). NOT rationale (that's Explanation).

Pages (revisit + finalize per R1 at claim time):
- docs/how-to/index.md — quadrant landing
- docs/how-to/daily-session-lifecycle.md (or split: open-a-session.md, claim-a-bead.md, finish-a-bead.md, stop-a-session.md — depends on R1's per-task granularity guidance)
- docs/how-to/bypass-workflow-ceremony.md — was §13
- docs/how-to/common-scenarios/ — was §14 (multi-task guides; possibly per-scenario sub-pages)
- docs/how-to/where-to-update-what.md — was §16

Acceptance:
- Each guide is a single task with clear precondition + steps + outcome.
- No first-person narrative voice.
- No exhaustive lists (link out to Reference for those).
- No 'why' content (link out to Explanation).
- Grep + cross-link audits clean.

Recipe shape: refactor-a-bead.
close_reason: Closed

title: Reference quadrant: migrate manual.md §2/§5-§12/§15 + glob auto-discovery
description:
Goal: Migrate the existing reference content from docs/manual.md (§2 + §5-§12 + §15) into Diataxis-shaped docs/reference/ pages. Set up mkdocs-include-markdown with glob patterns so primitive docs auto-track the filesystem (skills/, commands/, agents/, hooks/).

Diataxis discipline (refined by R1 drawer at claim): factual, third-person, terse. No narrative ('you might want to'), no rationale ('we decided to'), no step-by-step ('first do X then Y'). Audience: knows what they're looking for, needs an exact answer. Lists, tables, exact path names.

Pages (revisit + finalize per R1 at claim time):
- docs/reference/index.md — quadrant landing + ToC
- docs/reference/installed-files.md — was §2
- docs/reference/skills/ — chrome + auto-glob include of skills/*/SKILL.md
- docs/reference/slash-commands/ — chrome + auto-glob include of commands/*.md
- docs/reference/subagents/ — chrome + auto-glob include of agents/*.md
- docs/reference/hooks/ — chrome + auto-glob include of hooks/*.sh header comments
- docs/reference/path-scoped-rules.md — was §9
- docs/reference/bd-cli.md — was §10 (consider thinning to a link to beads' own docs)
- docs/reference/mempalace-mcp.md — was §11 (consider thinning to a link to mempalace's own docs)
- docs/reference/decision-tables.md — was §12
- docs/reference/glossary.md — was §15

Acceptance:
- Every primitive on disk auto-appears as a doc page (verify by adding a test file under skills/, building, removing, rebuilding).
- Adding/removing primitives requires NO nav.yml edits.
- Grep audit catches no first-person prose, no 'we decided', no narrative.
- Cross-link audit catches no broken refs.
- mkdocs build succeeds locally + via GH Actions.

Recipe shape: refactor-a-bead. Behavior preserved (content is the same; structure changes).
close_reason: Closed

title: bd pre-commit hook exports issues.jsonl to worktree root instead of .beads/
description:
The bd pre-commit hook (installed via .beads/hooks/pre-commit, which calls 'bd hooks run pre-commit') exports issues.jsonl to the WORKTREE ROOT when commit happens inside a git worktree (e.g. .worktrees/loom-qj3/), instead of (or in addition to) the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path.

**Symptom**: during the loom-qj3 commit (e7d853c), the export ran with this output: 'Exported 14 issues to /home/frank/repos/loom/.worktrees/loom-qj3/.beads/issues.jsonl' — but the resulting commit included a NEW tracked file at the worktree root: 'create mode 100644 issues.jsonl' (14 lines). The .beads/issues.jsonl path was also touched. So either bd exported to BOTH places, or the export message lied about the path.

**Reproduction**: from within a git worktree under .worktrees/<name>/, run a commit. Observe that 'git show HEAD --stat' includes a stray 'issues.jsonl' at root not just at .beads/issues.jsonl.

**Self-defeating cleanup**: attempting 'git rm issues.jsonl && git commit' to clean up produces an empty commit (b20ac95 in loom-qj3 worktree) — the pre-commit hook re-exports the file during the cleanup commit, re-staging it with the same content as HEAD, so the deletion gets reverted before the commit is captured. Net: the stray tracked-but-missing-on-disk file persists in HEAD across cleanup attempts.

**Impact**: cosmetic but annoying. The stray file shows as 'deleted' in 'git status' indefinitely (since HEAD has it but disk doesn't), creating perpetual visual noise. Doesn't affect bd dolt push or sync since those use the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path.

**Investigation pointers**:
- /home/frank/repos/loom/.beads/hooks/pre-commit (the bd-installed hook stub that calls 'bd hooks run pre-commit')
- bd CLI's worktree-detection logic (does 'bd hooks run pre-commit' detect git_dir vs worktree path correctly?)
- The 'Warning: .../.beads has permissions 0775 (recommended: 0700)' message that fires from worktrees but not from main repo — adjacent symptom suggesting bd's worktree handling may have multiple sharp edges.

**Proposed fix shape**: bd's pre-commit export logic should resolve the canonical .beads/issues.jsonl path via 'git rev-parse --show-toplevel' or equivalent, and write only there. If a stray issues.jsonl exists at any other path, leave it alone (don't re-export there).

**Workaround until fixed**: do the commit from the main repo path (cd /home/frank/repos/loom && git commit) instead of from the worktree, OR accept the stray file and clean it up via a manual cleanup commit AFTER the main bead has been merged + the worktree removed.

Out of scope: changing the canonical export path; only the worktree-context bug needs fixing.
close_reason: Defensive workaround shipped: /issues.jsonl in .gitignore (root-anchored, doesn't affect canonical .beads/issues.jsonl) + CLAUDE.md 'Don't' note recommending main-path commits over worktree-mode commits. Upstream fix in beads CLI's worktree-detection is a separate concern; loom only defends against the bug's footprint. commit a1bda4e.

title: v2: manual §5/§6 expansion + user-facing slash command inventory
description:
Follow-up to loom-qj3 (which scoped to §4 rewrite + new walkthrough, leaving these gaps).

Three docs/manual.md gaps surfaced during the loom-qj3 M4 review pass:

1. **§5 'Reference: skills' missing sibling recipe entries.** Today only `bugfix-a-bead`, `bead-lifecycle-shell`, `session-startup`, `audit-project` get individual entries. Add per-recipe entries for `feature-a-bead`, `refactor-a-bead`, `research-a-bead`, `cleanup-a-bead`, `docs-a-bead` so the reference section enumerates all installed skills.

2. **§6 'Reference: slash commands' missing entries + clear user-facing inventory.** Today only `/bugfix-a-bead`, `/lineage`, `/wrap-up`, `/audit-project` get entries. Missing: `/working-a-bead` (the router, shipped loom-1ab), `/research-a-bead` (shipped loom-0q0). Plus per Frank's request: a clear inventory section explicitly distinguishing user-facing slash commands (entry points like `/working-a-bead`, `/audit-project`, `/wrap-up`; shape-specific shortcuts like `/bugfix-a-bead`, `/research-a-bead`; diagnostic `/lineage`) from skills only invoked via Skill auto-discovery (the four sibling recipes that don't ship a direct slash command: `feature-a-bead`, `refactor-a-bead`, `cleanup-a-bead`, `docs-a-bead`) and from internal shell-only skills (`bead-lifecycle-shell` — never user-invoked, called by activity recipes).

3. **§5 line 432 stale count.** The loom-7z1 closing drawer flagged this: 'All three have `disable-model-invocation: true`' references slash commands but there are now 5 (`bugfix-a-bead`, `research-a-bead`, `lineage`, `wrap-up`, `audit-project` — `working-a-bead` is the 6th but explicitly intended to dispatch via Skill, so check whether it has the flag). Verify count + update.

Acceptance: §5 enumerates all 8 installed skills; §6 enumerates all 6 user-facing slash commands; a new sub-section under §6 (or a small table) makes the user-vs-internal distinction explicit; the line 432 count is correct.

Recipe shape: docs-a-bead. Voice/structure already established by loom-qj3's section-4 work — match that.

Out of scope: changes to actual slash command frontmatter or skill descriptions; this is documentation-only.
close_reason: §5 enumerates all 8 installed skills (added entries for feature/refactor/research/cleanup/docs-a-bead); §6 adds Inventory sub-section distinguishing user-facing slash commands (6) from Skill-auto-discovery-only recipes (4) from internal shell (bead-lifecycle-shell); §6 adds /research-a-bead + /working-a-bead entries; count fix 'All three' → 'All six' verified by grepping all 6 commands/*.md have disable-model-invocation. Caught §7 stale count too (3 → 4 subagents since project-onboarder shipped). commit on main.

title: v2: cleanup-a-bead recipe
description: skills/cleanup-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify scope (often via bd orphans + bd stale + grep for stale references) → remove → verify nothing broke (full test suite + manual smoke if integration). No new tests typically. Distinct from refactor: removal vs restructure. Many follow-up beads from HAW deploy-day fit this shape (80v already shipped via trimmed working-a-bead; future cleanups should use this recipe).
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: v2: manual + walkthrough updates for multi-recipe shape
description: docs/manual.md section 4 changes from 'The 14-step bead recipe' (singular) to 'The recipes' (plural). New per-recipe sub-sections summarize each. Author docs/feature-walkthrough.md as companion to the existing bug-shaped walkthrough — narrative session walking through a real feature bead so the recipe shape is demonstrable. Also confirm wiki mirrors stay in sync (Frank handles wiki sync manually per 2026-05-03).
close_reason: docs/manual.md §4 rewritten singular→plural with shared-shell intro + 6 per-recipe sub-sections; §3/§14/§15 consistency fixes for content directly contradicted by the new §4. New docs/feature-walkthrough.md (749 lines) walks loom-1ab via feature-a-bead, mirrors the bug walkthrough's structure. M4 review fixed 4 lying-doc bugs (slash-cmd over-claim, /feature-a-bead invalid invocation, stage-name typos, line-count mismatch). Out-of-scope items filed as loom-469; bd worktree-export hook bug filed as loom-22h. Merged main commit 10f7d22.

title: v2: docs-a-bead recipe
description: skills/docs-a-bead/SKILL.md. Activity-specific middle: identify gap (drift from code, missing context, stale section) → draft → review against code for accuracy → commit. No TDD beyond doctest/link-checking when applicable. The doc itself is the deliverable; capture is the doc, not a separate drawer (unless the doc captures a decision). Voice consistency matters.
close_reason: recipe SKILL.md authored, install.sh symlinks deployed and verified

title: git-push-bd-sync hook false-positive: tighten matcher when push target ≠ cwd .beads/
description: When pushing repo A from a cwd inside repo B with dirty .beads/, the git-push-bd-sync hook warns about B's beads even though the push is for A. Surfaced 2026-05-03 during loom extraction (multiple loom pushes triggered the warning because cwd was HAW with dirty .beads/). Fix: detect whether the dirty .beads/ belongs to the same repo as the push target. Only warn if the same. Use git rev-parse --show-toplevel + comparison with the remote URL git is pushing to. Document the new behavior in docs/manual.md hooks reference.
close_reason: Hook now resolves push target via cd-chain parsing; .beads/ presence and dirty-check run against target dir, not cwd. 11 fixture tests under lib/tests/. Docs updated. Light-mode close.

title: LOOM PROVENANCE — inherited from HAW workflow infrastructure work
description: Anchor bead documenting loom's origin. Forged 2026-05-03 via extraction from HAW (~/Working/hundred-acre-woods). Inherited closed beads from HAW: epic hundred-acre-woods-2st (workflow-infra v1, 8/9 children closed: 3jn working-a-bead skill, 5a9 session-startup extensions, 24a slash commands, ivg subagents, 91d hooks, 7z5 path-scoped rules, qi0 settings/permissions, bhs CLAUDE.md updates; deferred: vjb agent teams pilot — stays in HAW as Phase-4-related). Plus epic hundred-acre-woods-bng (v2): 7o9 shared shell, ipj rename plan, jnd opt-out + state + statusline, bmi audit-project. The active v2 epic in loom (loom-?) supersedes bng's still-open children. Cross-reference: HAW migration marker hundred-acre-woods-1qd. MemPalace drawer 'LOOM EXTRACTED FROM HAW' in hundred_acre_woods/decisions. KG triples: loom_repo → extracted_from → hundred-acre-woods_workflow_infrastructure_2026-05-03; loom_repo → github_url → github.com/fkberthold/loom; loom_repo → install_mechanism → symlinks_into_~/.claude/_via_install.sh_with_pre-loom.bak_backups.
close_reason: Anchor bead — captures loom's origin from HAW. Reference for future loom sessions.
